headline: Whistleblower Protection Act shields only 9 people since 2010
context: Since the Whistleblower Protection Act 2010 took effect, only nine individuals have received formal protection status, with four subsequently reporting retaliation.

cards.big: In 16 years, only 9 Malaysians received whistleblower protection
cards.sub: Four of those nine reported workplace retaliation despite legal safeguards

cards.big: Act excludes disclosures to media — only reports to enforcement qualify
cards.sub: Whistleblowers who go public first lose all statutory protection rights

cards.big: South Korea's equivalent law protected 2,400 whistleblowers in 2024 alone
cards.sub: Malaysia's rate is 0.6 per year versus South Korea's 2,400 per year

cards.big: No dedicated agency exists to receive or process whistleblower complaints
cards.sub: Reports go to the same agencies being complained about in 60% of cases

cards.big: A protection law that protects almost nobody is worse than no law — it creates false confidence
Should Malaysia establish an independent whistleblower protection commission?
